text: 67.1g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
11.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a nut, fat quality matters a ton.
14.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 38% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.
magnesium at 89% daily value, zinc at 40% daily value, iron at 14% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

on the plus side: texture helps them feel satisfying. that can make moderation easier than with softer snack foods. portable, practical snack food. nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety. they can hold up better than many snack foods.

the drawbacks next: easy to overdo in real portions. tiny serving sizes can make the practical use case awkward. cost or allergy issues can limit use. practical downsides still count. calorie density is very high. that makes portion control a real issue.
B tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
